title: 9分达人听力6 Test 4 Listening
source: 《9分达人雅思听力真题还原及解析6》

sections.title: Part 1
sections.source_title: Rented Properties Information About a House

groups.title: Rented Properties Information About a House
groups.desc:
Questions 1-10
Complete the notes below.
Write NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS AND/OR A NUMBER for each answer.
groups.collect: Available date: $1$ Prices: • rent: $2$ $ per month • deposit: $1,500 • $3$: $15 Facilities: • 3 bedrooms and 2 bathrooms • a remodelled $4$ • no $5$ • a $6$ with a work area Utilities: • The landlord will provide landscaping service, but the tenants must $7$ the grass. • The tenants should pay $15 for trashing and $8$ service. • The tenants should pay for electricity, water and gas bills. Other information: There is no central air conditioning, but there is a $9$ conditioning unit. Name of landlord: Sam $10$

questions.title: Available date: 【   】
questions.answer: 5(th) May / 5th May / 5 May / May 5(th) / May 5th / May 5
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 date；提示词 date，此处应填日期。
题目解析：定位词 date 出现后，先听到了干扰信息 May 3rd，注意这是房东与现租客合同到期的日子（… is due），接着房东说需要花一天时间来打扫和整理，随后出现的 5th May 才是房子可租住的日期，其中 be ready 对应题干中的 Available。因此答案为 5(th) May 或 May 5(th)。

questions.title: rent: 【   】 $ per month
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 rent；提示词 $，此处应填数字。
题目解析：本题考查对数字的反应速度以及对干扰信息的辨识。定位词 rent 首先出现在学生说的话中，随后出现了好几个数字，不过需要注意这都是学生自己所说，分别为自己的理想价位（每人每月 500 美元）和最高可承受价位（每人每月 600 美元），都是干扰信息。注意听说话者的角色，答案会出现在房东的话中。在学生说完后，房东对 rent 进行说明，这里给出了 1700 这个数字，为真正的房租租金。因此答案为 1700。

听前预测：定位词 $15；提示词 rent、deposit，此处应填名词，且与 rent 和 deposit 并列。
题目解析：本题答案前置，出现在定位词 $15 之前，如果死盯定位词可能会错过答案。通过之前的预判可知，本题答案应与 rent 和 deposit 并列，所以只要在 deposit 和 $1,500 出现后，有意识地听表示并列关系的逻辑衔接，然后抓取符合预判的词汇即可。本题可通过房东所说的 One important thing 这一表示强调的并列信号词锁定答案句：“… you'll be required to provide a credit check to guarantee your ability to pay the rent every month.”抓取其中符合预判的词汇即可，同时可通过之后出现的定位词 $15 进行确认。因此答案为 credit check。

questions.title: a remodelled 【   】
questions.analysis:
听前预测：提示词 remodelled，此处应填表示房间类型的名词。
题目解析：本题虽无好的定位词，但可通过上一个信息点 3 bedrooms and 2 bathrooms 来预判答案的出现，之后有意识地抓取表示房间类型的名词即可。答案句为：“Then there is a shared kitchen which was refurbished several months ago.”其中 refurbished 替换了题干 remodelled。因此答案为 kitchen。

questions.title: no 【   】
questions.analysis:
听前预测：提示词 no，此处应填名词。
题目解析：通过提示词 no 可知，本题的答案应该是被否定了的信息。通过学生对房东的回答“we like cooking”以及提问“Do we have to buy our own cookers?”可知，接下来讨论的内容有关厨房设备。答案信息由转折信号词 but 引出：“… but if you want a dishwasher, I'm sorry it needs to be added later.”其中 it needs to be added later 替换了题干中的 no。因此答案为 dishwasher。

questions.title: a 【   】 with a work area
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 work area；提示词 a，此处应填表示房间类型的名词。
题目解析：本题同样是答案前置，出现在定位词 work area 之前。建议通过学生的提问“Is there any place we can park?”去定位下一条信息的出现，房东随即给出答案：“Yes, there is a garage which not only provides space for your car but also has a special section where you can do some of your work.”抓取其中表示房间类型的词汇即可，再根据后面提到的 work area 的同义替换 a special section … do some of your work 进行确认。因此答案为 garage。

questions.title: the tenants must 【   】 the grass
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 landscaping、grass；提示词 must，此处应填动词原形。
题目解析：本题难度不大，定位词 landscaping 和 grass 均不容易替换。landscaping 出现后，通过转折信号词 But 锁定答案句：“But you do need to help water the grass once a week.”其中 need to 替换了提示词 must，比较容易识别。因此答案为 water。

questions.title: The tenants should pay $15 for trashing and 【   】 service.
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 $15、trashing；提示词 and，此处应填与 trashing 并列的名词。
题目解析：本题定位词 $15 出现于答案之后，且 trashing 在录音中被替换为 cleaning up the rubbish。能识别出这一同义替换即可通过并列连词 and 找到答案词，然后再通过 $15 进行验证即可。当然如果无法一下子识别出 trashing 的同义替换，也可以通过对话的语义推进来定位，接第 7 题之后出现了并列信号词 Another thing is，从而可锁定答案句即将出现，重点听并列出现的信息即可。因此答案为 recycling。

questions.title: there is a 【   】 conditioning unit
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 central air conditioning、conditioning unit；提示词 a、conditioning unit，此处应填名词或形容词。
题目解析：首先听到定位词 central air conditioning 原词重现，随后答案信息由转折信号词 but 引出：“… but each room has a conditioning unit which is installed on the window.”其中另一定位词 conditioning unit 也原词重现，可以修饰它的词只有名词 window。因此答案为 window。

questions.title: Name of landlord: Sam 【   】
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 Name；提示词 Name，此处应填人名。
题目解析：本题比较简单。雅思听力中考查人名时通常会给出拼写，所以注意跟上录音即可。本题的人名中有两个 s，读法为 double s。因此答案为 Dressler。

transcript.en: Landlord: Hello?
transcript.cn: 喂？

transcript.en: Student: Oh, hello.
transcript.cn: 哦，你好。

transcript.en: Student: I wanted to enquire about the house you're going to let out.
transcript.cn: 我想咨询一下你要出租的那套房子。

transcript.en: Student: Is it still available?
transcript.cn: 现在还能租吗？

transcript.en: Landlord: Yes.
transcript.cn: 能租。

transcript.en: Landlord: May I ask where you heard about it?
transcript.cn: 我能问一下你是从哪儿知道这套房子的吗？

transcript.en: Landlord: Was it in an advert?
transcript.cn: 是在广告上看到的吗？

transcript.en: Student: Right, it was.
transcript.cn: 对，就是在广告上看到的。

transcript.en: Landlord: OK, what do you want to know?
transcript.cn: 好的，你想了解些什么？

transcript.en: Student: Well, I'm going to share the house with two friends.
transcript.cn: 是这样的，我打算和两个朋友合租这套房子。

transcript.en: Student: We've been staying on campus, but we're going to graduate, so we need to find a house as soon as possible.
transcript.cn: 我们一直住在学校里，但快毕业了，所以得尽快找个房子。

transcript.en: Student: Anyway, could you tell me the exact date when we can move in?
transcript.cn: 对了，你能告诉我我们具体从哪天可以搬进去吗？

transcript.en: Landlord: Well, the contract with my current tenant is due next week, on May 3rd.
transcript.cn: 嗯，我和现在租客的合同下周就到期了，是5月3号。

transcript.en: Landlord: And I need a day to clean and check the things in the house, so I think the house will be ready on 5th May.
transcript.cn: 我还得花一天时间打扫、检查一下房子里的东西，所以我想房子5月5号就能准备好了。

transcript.en: Landlord: Is that OK?
transcript.cn: 这样可以吗？

transcript.en: Student: Yes, that will be fine.
transcript.cn: 可以，那没问题。

transcript.en: Student: And what we're most concerned about is the rent.
transcript.cn: 还有我们最在意的就是房租。

transcript.en: Student: We'd like something around 500 dollars a month for each of us, but we could go up to 600 dollars if we have to.
transcript.cn: 我们希望每人每月500美元左右，实在不行的话最多可以到600美元。

transcript.en: Student: But we can't go beyond that.
transcript.cn: 但再高就不行了。

transcript.en: Landlord: You are lucky – the rent will be 1,700 dollars in total, which is within your budget.
transcript.cn: 你们很走运——房租总共是1700美元，在你们的预算范围内。

transcript.en: Landlord: You'll need to pay monthly to me, by cash or credit card, I don't mind.
transcript.cn: 你们要按月付给我，付现金或者刷信用卡都行，我无所谓。

transcript.en: Landlord: And you'll have to pay me 1,500 dollars as a deposit before you move in.
transcript.cn: 另外你们搬进来之前得先付给我1500美元作为押金。

transcript.en: Landlord: The deposit is as insurance, in case you break something.
transcript.cn: 押金相当于一种保障，万一你们弄坏了什么东西可以用。

transcript.en: Student: OK, that's reasonable.
transcript.cn: 好的，这挺合理的。

transcript.en: Landlord: Oh, I nearly forgot.
transcript.cn: 哦，我差点忘了。

transcript.en: Landlord: One important thing is that you'll be required to provide a credit check to guarantee your ability to pay the rent every month.
transcript.cn: 有件重要的事，就是你们需要提供一份信用检查，用来证明你们每个月都有能力付房租。

transcript.en: Landlord: This will charge you 15 dollars.
transcript.cn: 这个要收你们15美元。

transcript.en: Student: No problem.
transcript.cn: 没问题。

transcript.en: Student: How about the facilities?
transcript.cn: 那房子的设施怎么样？

transcript.en: Landlord: First there are three bedrooms combined with two bathrooms.
transcript.cn: 首先有三间卧室配两个卫生间。

transcript.en: Landlord: Then there is a shared kitchen which was refurbished several months ago.
transcript.cn: 然后有一个公用厨房，几个月前刚翻新过。

transcript.en: Landlord: By the way, do you cook yourselves?
transcript.cn: 顺便问一下，你们自己做饭吗？

transcript.en: Student: Yeah, we like cooking.
transcript.cn: 做的，我们喜欢做饭。

transcript.en: Student: Do we have to buy our own cookers?
transcript.cn: 那我们得自己买灶具吗？

transcript.en: Landlord: Basic facilities for cooking are available, but if you want a dishwasher, I'm sorry it needs to be added later.
transcript.cn: 做饭的基本设施都有，不过如果你们想要洗碗机的话，抱歉，那得以后再添了。

transcript.en: Student: I think the basic facilities are enough for us.
transcript.cn: 我觉得基本设施对我们来说就够了。

transcript.en: Student: Is there any place we can park?
transcript.cn: 有地方可以停车吗？

transcript.en: Student: As one of my friends has a private car.
transcript.cn: 因为我有个朋友有私家车。

transcript.en: Landlord: Yes, there is a garage which not only provides space for your car but also has a special section where you can do some of your work.
transcript.cn: 有的，有一个车库，不但能停车，还有一块专门的区域可以让你们干点活儿。

transcript.en: Landlord: It's very quiet for you to concentrate.
transcript.cn: 那里很安静，方便你们专心做事。

transcript.en: Student: It sounds so special!
transcript.cn: 听起来太特别了！

transcript.en: Landlord: There are some other utilities I have to tell you.
transcript.cn: 还有一些其他的配套我得跟你们说一下。

transcript.en: Landlord: There is a garden in front of the house, in which you will find some interesting plants and beautiful flowers.
transcript.cn: 房子前面有个花园，里面有一些有意思的植物和漂亮的花。

transcript.en: Student: Do we have to take care of these plants and flowers?
transcript.cn: 这些植物和花需要我们来打理吗？

transcript.en: Landlord: Well, you don't have to worry about that.
transcript.cn: 这个你们不用操心。

transcript.en: Landlord: There will be a professional gardener to do the landscaping in the garden regularly.
transcript.cn: 会有专业的园丁定期来做园林养护。

transcript.en: Landlord: But you do need to help water the grass once a week.
transcript.cn: 但你们确实需要每周帮忙给草坪浇一次水。

transcript.en: Landlord: Is it a problem for you?
transcript.cn: 这对你们来说有问题吗？

transcript.en: Student: Of course not.
transcript.cn: 当然没问题。

transcript.en: Student: We'd like to do that.
transcript.cn: 我们很乐意做这个。

transcript.en: Landlord: Another thing is, in this district every household is arranged with a cleaner responsible for cleaning up the rubbish and recycling, but this service will cost you 15 dollars every month.
transcript.cn: 还有一件事，这个片区每户都会安排一名清洁工负责清理垃圾和回收，不过这项服务每月要收你们15美元。

transcript.en: Student: What other bills do we have to pay?
transcript.cn: 我们还要付哪些账单呢？

transcript.en: Landlord: Bills for electricity, water and gas will also be covered by yourselves.
transcript.cn: 电费、水费和燃气费也要你们自己承担。

transcript.en: Student: OK.
transcript.cn: 好的。

transcript.en: Student: One more question, is air-conditioning available in the house?
transcript.cn: 还有一个问题，房子里有空调吗？

transcript.en: Landlord: If you mean central air-conditioning, I'm afraid there isn't at the moment, but each room has a conditioning unit which is installed on the window.
transcript.cn: 如果你说的是中央空调，恐怕现在是没有的，不过每个房间的窗户上都装了一台空调。

transcript.en: Student: That will be fine.
transcript.cn: 那就行了。

transcript.en: Student: Anyway, I think it's a good place to live in.
transcript.cn: 总之，我觉得这是个不错的居住地方。

transcript.en: Student: Can we go and see the house, please?
transcript.cn: 我们能去看看房子吗？

transcript.en: Landlord: Yes, of course.
transcript.cn: 当然可以。

transcript.en: Student: May I have your name and phone number?
transcript.cn: 能给我你的名字和电话号码吗？

transcript.en: Landlord: Yes, I'm Sam Dressler.
transcript.cn: 可以，我叫Sam Dressler。

transcript.en: Landlord: That's S-A-M, D-R-E-double S-L-E-R.
transcript.cn: 拼作S-A-M，D-R-E，两个S，L-E-R。

transcript.en: Student: OK, Sam Dressler, I've got that down.
transcript.cn: 好的，Sam Dressler，我记下来了。

transcript.en: Landlord: And my phone number is 6753 4571.
transcript.cn: 我的电话号码是6753 4571。

transcript.en: Student: Thank you very much.
transcript.cn: 非常感谢。

transcript.en: Student: I'll contact you after discussing it with my two friends.
transcript.cn: 我和两个朋友商量一下再联系你。

transcript.en: Landlord: No problem.
transcript.cn: 没问题。

transcript.en: Landlord: Bye!
transcript.cn: 再见！

transcript.en: Student: Bye!
transcript.cn: 再见！

groups.desc:
Questions 11-16
Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

questions.title: When does the riding in the park open?
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 riding；提示词 open。
题目解析：本题难度不大，注意题干问的是 riding 开始的时间，而不是公园开放的时间。首先听到了 9.30，对应的是公园开放的时间，为干扰信息。随后听到了定位词 riding，答案信息随即出现："The actual riding begins at 10 am."其中 riding 再次出现，begins 替换了题干中的 open。因此答案为 C。

questions.title: How much does a Family-fast-line Track ticket cost if you book online?
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 Family-fast-line Track ticket；提示词 online。
题目解析：题干的条件限定为 online，也就是不仅要听到 Family-fast-line Track ticket，还要满足 online 这一条件。定位词 Family-fast-line Track ticket 第一次出现时距离答案信息较远，需耐心等待。之后首先听到了 89 和 78，不过这都是 Basic ticket 的价格，可排除。接着定位词 Family-fast-line Track ticket 再次出现，意味着答案信息即将出现："For the Family-fast-line Track ticket, you only have to pay 95 pounds on the Internet instead of its original price 113 pounds."其中 on the Internet 替换了提示词 online，而 113 对应的是 original price，不符合 online 这一限定，也可通过否定信号词 instead of 直接排除 113。因此答案为 B。

questions.title: What is suggested when you plan to go to the lake in the park?
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 lake；提示词 suggested。
题目解析：定位词 lake 出现得较早，在两三句话之后才听到了选项 C 中的 waterproof 和选项 A 中的 camera，但这两个选项很容易排除，录音中分别用 Forget about 和 don't 对这两个选项进行了否定。随后听到了答案句："As a result, I strongly advise that the best time to visit the lake is in the hottest season, in case you catch a cold."其中 advise 替换了提示词 suggested，the hottest season 对应选项 B 中的 summer。因此答案为 B。

questions.title: What is special about the rollercoaster bike?

options.title: B
options.content: It is the most exciting activity.

questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 rollercoaster bike；提示词 special。
题目解析：定位词 rollercoaster bike 原词重现，但需要听出提示词 special 被替换为 different from the normal one 才可找到答案句："This rollercoaster bike is different from the normal one in that it's made of wood, so it's lighter."其中 wood 为选项 A 中 special materials 的具体展开替换。注意排除选项 B，录音中只提到了 exciting，并无 most 修饰，并排除选项 C，录音中虽提及 families，但只是建议家庭去尝试一下，并未提及是为家庭而设计。因此答案为 A。

questions.title: What should they bring to the resort?
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 resort；提示词 should ... bring。
题目解析：定位词 resort 原词重现，但所在句并非答案句。能听出提示词 should ... bring 的同义替换 have to prepare 即可定位到答案句："Please remember, this section is for a picnic, so you have to prepare your own snacks."其中 snacks 为选项 A 中 food 的具体展开替换，且选项中的 picnic 也曾出现。选项 B 为无中生有，录音虽提及 taking photos，但未提及带 cameras，而选项 C 可通过否定表达 don't worry about 排除。因此答案为 A。

questions.title: What activity is recommended toward the end of a day trip?
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 toward the end of a day trip；提示词 recommended。
题目解析：首先听到了选项 C 中的 circus 和选项 B 中的 fishing，但这两个选项是作为举例并列出现的，从逻辑上便可排除。接着通过定位词锁定答案句："At the end of the one-day trip, there is a special event for you – the firework display."其中 the firework display 对应选项 A watching fireworks，且随后出现的 a must 替换了提示词 recommended。因此答案为 A。

groups.title: Activities
groups.question_title: What comment is made about each of the following activities?
groups.desc:
Questions 17-20
Choose FOUR answers from the box and write the correct letter, A-G, next to Questions 17-20.

list.title: E
list.content: is based on a real story

questions.title: Cowboy Show
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 Cowboy Show。
题目解析：本部分 4 道题的题干均为大写的活动名称，不会发生替换，精确定位后重点听选项的同义替换即可。定位词 Cowboy Show 原词重现后，首先听到选项 E 中 story 的复数形式 stories，但需要注意录音中并无 real 修饰，信息并非完全对应，故排除选项 E。随后听到答案句："Rest assured that you don't need to queue for hours to get in."其中 queue for hours 替换了选项 A 中的 have a long wait。因此答案为 A。

questions.title: Driving School Ride
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 Driving School Ride。
题目解析：定位词 Driving School Ride 原词重现后，首先提到在这项活动中可以尝试多种交通工具，随后答案句由转折信号词 But 引出："But please note that this one is only for those below 1.2 metres."其中 those below 1.2 metres 对应了选项 C 中的 children，且后文中也提到了 kids。因此答案为 C。

questions.title: Rollercoaster Jumping
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 Rollercoaster Jumping。
题目解析：定位词 Rollercoaster Jumping 原词重现后，随即出现答案信息："... it is the scariest activity, as you will experience the vertical dropping, ..."其中 the scariest 和 vertical dropping 都对应了选项 F 中的 the most frightening。注意排除干扰项 C，录音提到 children 时用了 not allowed 进行否定。因此答案为 F。

questions.title: Magic Show
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 Magic Show。
题目解析：定位词 Magic Show 出现得较早，需耐心往下听。答案句由 Please promise me 引出："... don't leave early because just as horror stories won't tell you until the end, there is something unexpected waiting for you."其中 end 对应选项 G 中的 ending，something unexpected 替换了 surprising。注意 horror stories won't tell you until the end 是一个比喻句，表示"惊喜总在最后出现"，对应选项 G，而非选项 F。因此答案为 G。

transcript.en: Welcome to our radio programme Travel with Me.
transcript.cn: 欢迎收听我们的广播节目《跟我去旅行》。

transcript.en: Today I'll recommend you a fantastic place – it's the theme park in Fun City.
transcript.cn: 今天我要给大家推荐一个超棒的地方——就是欢乐城里的主题公园。

transcript.en: It has been awarded the most popular tourist attraction in the city, so just follow me to find something interesting there.
transcript.cn: 它被评为这座城市最受欢迎的旅游景点，所以就跟着我一起去看看那里有什么好玩的吧。

transcript.en: The park opens at 9.30 every morning.
transcript.cn: 公园每天早上9点半开门。

transcript.en: For the first activity, I definitely advise you shouldn't miss the riding in the park.
transcript.cn: 第一个项目，我强烈建议你们千万别错过园内的骑乘项目。

transcript.en: The actual riding begins at 10 am.
transcript.cn: 真正的骑乘项目上午10点才开始。

transcript.en: But I suggest you arrive a little earlier before the opening time; otherwise, there might be a long wait.
transcript.cn: 不过我建议你们在开门前早点到，不然可能要排很长时间的队。

transcript.en: And how much, you may ask, does an entrance ticket cost?
transcript.cn: 你们可能会问，门票要多少钱呢？

transcript.en: Well, there are two types of tickets: Basic ticket and Family-fast-line Track ticket.
transcript.cn: 是这样的，门票有两种：基础票和家庭快速通道票。

transcript.en: You can either buy a ticket at the ticket office near the main entrance to the park, or reserve from the official website in advance.
transcript.cn: 你们可以在公园正门附近的售票处买票，也可以提前在官网上预订。

transcript.en: The latter is preferable as it always offers a discount.
transcript.cn: 后者更划算，因为网上总会有折扣。

transcript.en: For example, the original price for a Basic ticket is 89 pounds, but if you buy it online, it only costs 78 pounds.
transcript.cn: 比如说，基础票原价是89英镑，但要是在网上买，只要78英镑。

transcript.en: For the Family-fast-line Track ticket, you only have to pay 95 pounds on the Internet instead of its original price 113 pounds.
transcript.cn: 家庭快速通道票在网上只要付95英镑，而原价是113英镑。

transcript.en: Visiting the lake in the park is also a good choice.
transcript.cn: 去公园里的湖边看看也是个不错的选择。

transcript.en: There is a new refreshment room beside the lake, and the latest thing – it was only finished last month – is the Splash Ride.
transcript.cn: 湖边有一个新建的休息室，最新的项目——上个月才刚建好的——就是水花滑道。

transcript.en: It is a new area and pretty exciting.
transcript.cn: 这是个新区域，相当刺激。

transcript.en: Forget about wearing waterproofs because you'll still get wet, and don't take cameras either.
transcript.cn: 别指望穿雨衣管用，因为你还是会被淋湿，另外也别带相机。

transcript.en: As a result, I strongly advise that the best time to visit the lake is in the hottest season, in case you catch a cold.
transcript.cn: 所以我强烈建议，游湖最好选在最热的季节，免得着凉感冒。

transcript.en: Another activity you'll find exciting is riding the rollercoaster bike.
transcript.cn: 另一个你会觉得很刺激的项目是骑过山车自行车。

transcript.en: I suggest families with children go for a try, as it's always favoured by them.
transcript.cn: 我建议带孩子的家庭去试试，因为它一直很受他们欢迎。

transcript.en: This rollercoaster bike is different from the normal one in that it's made of wood, so it's lighter.
transcript.cn: 这种过山车自行车和普通的不一样，它是木头做的，所以更轻。

transcript.en: The person who designed it has won a special prize for innovation.
transcript.cn: 设计它的人还因为这个创新拿了一个特别奖。

transcript.en: For those who are not interested in these exciting activities, there is a resort for you.
transcript.cn: 对于那些对这些刺激项目不感兴趣的人，还有一个休闲区可以去。

transcript.en: You can sit there talking, relaxing or taking photos.
transcript.cn: 你们可以坐在那里聊天、放松或者拍照。

transcript.en: Please remember, this section is for a picnic, so you have to prepare your own snacks.
transcript.cn: 请记住，这个区域是用来野餐的，所以你们得自己准备零食。

transcript.en: But don't worry about drinks, since free bottled water is available there.
transcript.cn: 不过饮料倒不用担心，那里提供免费的瓶装水。

transcript.en: Oh, one more thing is, it's better to take a picnic blanket to sit on.
transcript.cn: 哦，还有一件事，最好带一块野餐垫坐。

transcript.en: After the picnic, if there is some time left, you can take part in some other interesting activities you like, such as watching the circus or going fishing.
transcript.cn: 野餐之后，如果还有时间，你们可以去参加一些自己喜欢的其他有趣活动，比如看马戏或者钓鱼。

transcript.en: At the end of the one-day trip, there is a special event for you – the firework display.
transcript.cn: 在这一天行程的最后，还有一个特别的项目等着你们——烟花表演。

transcript.en: You'll enjoy a spectacular show in the sky.
transcript.cn: 你们会欣赏到一场壮观的天空表演。

transcript.en: Everyone is cheering.
transcript.cn: 所有人都在欢呼。

transcript.en: I think it's a must for you, with a perfect ending of the day.
transcript.cn: 我觉得这个你们一定不能错过，给这一天画上一个完美的句号。

transcript.en: OK, that was something about the most basic information, and now here's some more practical information about four popular activities on the brochure of the theme park, because some people want to know whether they're worth trying.
transcript.cn: 好，以上就是一些最基本的信息，现在再给大家讲一些更实用的内容，是关于这个主题公园宣传册上四个热门项目的，因为有些人想知道这些项目值不值得一试。

transcript.en: Well, let's first look at the Cowboy Show.
transcript.cn: 好，我们先来看看牛仔秀。

transcript.en: It's a dance show by a group of boys wearing cowboy boots and hats.
transcript.cn: 这是一群穿着牛仔靴、戴着牛仔帽的男孩表演的舞蹈秀。

transcript.en: What they perform tells the stories of cowboys' life.
transcript.cn: 他们表演的内容讲的是牛仔的生活故事。

transcript.en: Rest assured that you don't need to queue for hours to get in.
transcript.cn: 放心，你们不用排上几个小时的队才能进去。

transcript.en: So please come and enjoy it.
transcript.cn: 所以快来欣赏吧。

transcript.en: Then there is the Driving School Ride.
transcript.cn: 接下来是驾驶学校游乐项目。

transcript.en: In this activity, you can try a variety of vehicles.
transcript.cn: 在这个项目里，你们可以尝试各种各样的车辆。

transcript.en: But please note that this one is only for those below 1.2 metres.
transcript.cn: 但请注意，这个项目只面向身高1.2米以下的人。

transcript.en: The staff will measure your height at the entrance.
transcript.cn: 工作人员会在入口处量你们的身高。

transcript.en: Parents can just have a rest on the chair and watch their kids playing.
transcript.cn: 家长可以坐在椅子上休息，看着孩子玩。

transcript.en: You don't have to be worried about safety, as everyone is guided by a coach throughout the process.
transcript.cn: 你们不用担心安全问题，因为全程都有教练指导。

transcript.en: As to Rollercoaster Jumping, it is the scariest activity, as you will experience the vertical dropping, so it might be a good choice for those who like extreme sports.
transcript.cn: 至于过山车跳跃，这是最吓人的项目，因为你会体验垂直下坠，所以对于喜欢极限运动的人来说可能是个不错的选择。

transcript.en: Of course, children are not allowed to join in.
transcript.cn: 当然，儿童是不允许参加的。

transcript.en: The last one is the Magic Show.
transcript.cn: 最后一个是魔术秀。

transcript.en: I'm sure you'll be attracted by the charm of the magician.
transcript.cn: 我敢肯定你们会被魔术师的魅力吸引住。

transcript.en: You can hear the audience burst into applause throughout the show.
transcript.cn: 整场表演中你都能听到观众爆发出的掌声。

transcript.en: Please promise me don't leave early because just as horror stories won't tell you until the end, there is something unexpected waiting for you.
transcript.cn: 请答应我别提前离场，因为就像恐怖故事总是留到最后才揭晓一样，有一个意想不到的惊喜在等着你们。

transcript.en: I'm sure you won't regret it.
transcript.cn: 我保证你们不会后悔的。

transcript.en: OK, that's all I want to recommend to you today.
transcript.cn: 好，以上就是我今天想给大家推荐的全部内容。

transcript.en: If you want to know about more fascinating tourist attractions, please wait for our programme next week.
transcript.cn: 如果你们还想了解更多迷人的旅游景点，请期待我们下周的节目。

transcript.en: Bye!
transcript.cn: 再见！

sections.source_title: Reasons for rejection during the application process

groups.question_title: Which TWO aspects should people just graduating from secondary schools emphasise?
groups.desc:
Questions 21 and 22
Choose TWO letters, A-E.

list.title: E
list.content: other areas of interests outside work

questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 secondary schools；提示词 emphasise。
题目解析：定位词 secondary schools 出现后，先后听到提示词 emphasise 的同义替换 focus on 和与选项 D 中 education 相关的信息出现在学生的提问中，但导师以 the degree is not that important 予以否定，the degree 对应选项 D 的 educational qualifications，故排除。接着听到了选项 B practical experience 的同义替换 work experience，但此处提及的工作经验与高中毕业生无关，且随后出现了转折信号词 However，从而可排除选项 B，重点听该之后的内容。之后的内容中夹杂了一个否定结构 neither … nor … 连接的选项 D 和 B，由此确定这两个选项都不是正确答案，真正的答案信息为：“… secondary school graduates should stress that they have great enthusiasm for work. Employees who are interested in their job …”其中 stress 替换了提示词 emphasise，great enthusiasm 和 are interested in 替换了选项 A 的 degree of motivation（积极性），故选项 A 正确。而选项 E 偷换概念，这句话只提到了对工作感兴趣，并未提及其他工作之外的领域，可排除。之后学生的提问“What else should they highlight …”，highlight 替换了提示词 emphasise，暗示另一个答案即将出现，注意听导师的回答：“… it is wise for them to demonstrate that they possess valuable characteristics such as curiosity, willingness to learn new stuff and good interpersonal skills.”其中 willingness to learn new stuff 替换了选项 C 的 eagerness to learn，故选项 C 正确。因此答案为 A & C。

groups.question_title: Which TWO of the following are mentioned about women who returned to work after raising children?
groups.desc:
Questions 23 and 24
Choose TWO letters, A-E.

list.title: B
list.content: They are more expensive to retrain.

list.title: E
list.content: They have difficulty in re-adjusting to work.

questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 women、after raising children。
题目解析：本题定位词原词重现，定位后首先听到了与选项 A 中 working time 相关的信息：“… Changes have to be made within the family to ensure they have sufficient working hours.”不过这句以及之前那句都是在说需要照顾小孩的女性应当如何调整在家庭和工作上的时间，以取得二者之间的平衡，并没有说她们在工作上时间有限（limited），属于偷换概念，可排除。两三句无效信息后，听到：“Even though they're quite confident in themselves, they often find it hard to adapt to these changes.”其中 quite confident in themselves 与选项 D 中的 lack confidence 矛盾，hard to adapt to 替换了选项 E 中的 difficulty in re-adjusting，故选项 D 错误，选项 E 正确。最后听到了由 But on the other hand 引导出的另一正确答案信息：“… many employers find these mature women valuable assets in the workplace.”其中出现了选项 C 中的 mature，故选项 C 正确。选项 B 在录音中导师并未提及。因此答案为 C & E。

听前预测：定位词 women、after raising children。
题目解析：本题定位词原词重现，定位后首先听到了与选项 A 中 working time 相关的信息：“… Changes have to be made within the family to ensure they have sufficient working hours.”不过这句以及之前那句都是在说需要照顾小孩的女性应当如何调整在家庭和工作上的时间，以取得二者之间的平衡，并没有说她们在工作上时间有限（limited），属于偷换概念，可排除。两三句无效信息后，听到：“Even though they're quite confident in themselves, they often find it hard to adapt to these changes.”其中 quite confident in themselves 与选项 D 中的 lack confidence 矛盾，hard to adapt to 替换了选项 E 中的 difficulty in re-adjusting，故选项 D 错误，选项 E 正确。最后听到了由 But on the other hand 引导出的另一正确答案信息：“… many employers find these mature women valuable assets in the workplace.”其中出现了选项 C 中的 mature，故选项 C 正确。选项 B 在录音中导师并未提及。因此答案为 C & E。

groups.question_title: Which TWO types of people do employers always avoid hiring?
groups.desc:
Questions 25 and 26
Choose TWO letters, A-E.

list.title: A
list.content: people who have worked for a rival company

list.title: B
list.content: people who have worked for their previous company for a long time

list.title: C
list.content: people who were sacked by a previous company

list.title: D
list.content: people who have been unemployed for a long time

list.title: E
list.content: people who quit their job because it was unsuitable

questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 employers；提示词 avoid hiring。
题目解析：题干的条件限定为 avoid hiring，审题时可标画“↓”箭头，重点听负向态度信息。本题定位词 employers 在录音中被替换为 companies，负向态度信息 unwelcoming 符合题干目标信息方向，之后首先听到选项 A 中的 a rival company 出现在了提问中，接着导师的回答是 not a taboo（并非禁忌），即使不知道 taboo 的意思，也可通过随后出现的 always welcomed 这一积极态度信息排除选项 A。接着导师又说：“What concerns employers the most is how they left the previous company … If they have been fired … must have done something wrong …”其中 concerns … the most 呼应题干负向态度信息提示词，fired 替换了选项 C 中的 sacked，故选项 C 正确。同时可排除选项 B，该选项信息不完整，长时间就职于上一家公司不会成为未被雇佣的因素，而离开这家公司的原因才是雇主关心的事情。之后学生的提问中出现了选项 D，注意导师的回答是 isn't at the bottom of the employment list，双重否定表示肯定，即未必不会聘用这类员工，排除选项 D。最后听到：“Most companies cast doubts on applicants who left the job simply because they didn't like …”其中 cast doubts on 呼应题干负向态度信息提示词，left 替换了选项 E 中的 quit，they didn't like the working hours or the work assigned 为 unsuitable 的具体展开替换，故选项 E 正确。因此答案为 C & E。

听前预测：定位词 employers；提示词 avoid hiring。
题目解析：题干的条件限定为 avoid hiring，审题时可标画“↓”箭头，重点听负向态度信息。本题定位词 employers 在录音中被替换为 companies，负向态度信息 unwelcoming 符合题干目标信息方向，之后首先听到选项 A 中的 a rival company 出现在了提问中，接着导师的回答是 not a taboo（并非禁忌），即使不知道 taboo 的意思，也可通过随后出现的 always welcomed 这一积极态度信息排除选项 A。接着导师又说：“What concerns employers the most is how they left the previous company … If they have been fired … must have done something wrong …”其中 concerns … the most 呼应题干负向态度信息提示词，fired 替换了选项 C 中的 sacked，故选项 C 正确。同时可排除选项 B，该选项信息不完整，长时间就职于上一家公司不会成为未被雇佣的因素，而离开这家公司的原因才是雇主关心的事情。之后学生的提问中出现了选项 D，注意导师的回答是 isn't at the bottom of the employment list，双重否定表示肯定，即未必不会聘用这类员工，排除选项 D。最后听到：“Most companies cast doubts on applicants who left the job simply because they didn't like …”其中 cast doubts on 呼应题干负向态度信息提示词，left 替换了选项 E 中的 quit，they didn't like the working hours or the work assigned 为 unsuitable 的具体展开替换，故选项 E 正确。因此答案为 C & E。

groups.question_title: Which THREE factors are mentioned as reasons why people avoid hiring retrenched workers?

list.title: A
list.content: lack of experience relevant to new industries

list.title: F
list.content: the reputation of the previous company

questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 retrenched workers；提示词 avoid hiring。
题目解析：题干的条件限定为 avoid hiring，审题时可标画“↓”箭头，重点听负向态度信息。导师的提问引出 worker retrenchment 相关信息，但未直接说 avoid hiring 的原因，而是学生先介绍了自己了解的情况并提出问题，负向态度信息 reluctant 才出现在导师的回答中，这时才正式进入主题。首先听到学生提到选项 D 中的 work experience，但以 must have plenty of 这一肯定语气出现，故排除选项 D。学生问在之前行业丰富的工作经验难道不算优势吗？导师的回答中出现了转折信号词 but，注意听之后的内容：“… but retrenched workers know nothing about other industries.”其中 know nothing about other industries 替换了选项 A 的 lack of experience relevant to new industries，故选项 A 正确。随后听到了选项 B 中的 costs，不过注意录音中提到的是 retrain（再培训），与选项 B 中的 employment（雇佣）意思不同，排除选项 B。接着学生提到政府会给这些员工提供培训项目，导师给予了肯定，随后出现了转折信号词 But 提示有特殊情况出现：“But retrenched workers are generally too old.”其中 generally too old 与选项 C 的 comparatively old age 相符，故选项 C 正确。之后又做出了更详细的解释：“They are also reluctant to adjust and are unwilling to be retrained.”其中 reluctant to adjust 和 unwilling to be retrained 符合选项 E 中的 the difficulty of retraining，故选项 E 正确。最后在学生的提问中听到了选项 F 中 reputation 的同义替换 big names，但导师随即给出了否定回答，可排除。选项 G 在这段内容中并未提及。因此答案为 A & C & E。

题目解析：题干的条件限定为 avoid hiring，审题时可标画“↓”箭头，重点听负向态度信息。导师的提问引出 worker retrenchment 相关信息，但未直接说 avoid hiring 的原因，而是学生先介绍了自己了解的情况并提出问题，负向态度信息 reluctant 才出现在导师的回答中，这时才正式进入主题。首先听到学生提到选项 D 中的 work experience，但以 must have plenty of 这一肯定语气出现，故排除选项 D。学生问在之前行业丰富的工作经验难道不算优势吗？导师的回答中出现了转折信号词 but，注意听之后的内容：“… but retrenched workers know nothing about other industries.”其中 know nothing about other industries 替换了选项 A 的 lack of experience relevant to new industries，故选项 A 正确。随后听到了选项 B 中的 costs，不过注意录音中提到的是 retrain（再培训），与选项 B 中的 employment（雇佣）意思不同，排除选项 B。接着学生提到政府会给这些员工提供培训项目，导师给予了肯定，随后出现了转折信号词 But 提示有特殊情况出现：“But retrenched workers are generally too old.”其中 generally too old 与选项 C 的 comparatively old age 相符，故选项 C 正确。之后又做出了更详细的解释：“They are also reluctant to adjust and are unwilling to be retrained.”其中 reluctant to adjust 和 unwilling to be retrained 符合选项 E 中的 the difficulty of retraining，故选项 E 正确。最后在学生的提问中听到了选项 F 中 reputation 的同义替换 big names，但导师随即给出了否定回答，可排除。选项 G 在这段内容中并未提及。因此答案为 A & C & E。

听前预测：定位词 retrenched workers；提示词 avoid hiring。
题目解析：题干的条件限定为 avoid hiring，审题时可标画“↓”箭头，重点听负向态度信息。导师的提问引出 worker retrenchment 相关信息，但未直接说 avoid hiring 的原因，而是学生先介绍了自己了解的情况并提出问题，负向态度信息 reluctant 才出现在导师的回答中，这时才正式进入主题。首先听到学生提到选项 D 中的 work experience，但以 must have plenty of 这一肯定语气出现，故排除选项 D。学生问在之前行业丰富的工作经验难道不算优势吗？导师的回答中出现了转折信号词 but，注意听之后的内容：“… but retrenched workers know nothing about other industries.”其中 know nothing about other industries 替换了选项 A 的 lack of experience relevant to new industries，故选项 A 正确。随后听到了选项 B 中的 costs，不过注意录音中提到的是 retrain（再培训），与选项 B 中的 employment（雇佣）意思不同，排除选项 B。接着学生提到政府会给这些员工提供培训项目，导师给予了肯定，随后出现了转折信号词 But 提示有特殊情况出现：“But retrenched workers are generally too old.”其中 generally too old 与选项 C 的 comparatively old age 相符，故选项 C 正确。之后又做出了更详细的解释：“They are also reluctant to adjust and are unwilling to be retrained.”其中 reluctant to adjust 和 unwilling to be retrained 符合选项 E 中的 the difficulty of retraining，故选项 E 正确。最后在学生的提问中听到了选项 F 中 reputation 的同义替换 big names，但导师随即给出了否定回答，可排除。选项 G 在这段内容中并未提及。因此答案为 A & C & E。

groups.title: Reasons for rejection during the application process
groups.desc:
Question 30
Complete the notes below.
Write ONE WORD ONLY for the answer.
groups.collect:
• unsuitable experience and skills
• applicants' inappropriate $30$ and appearance
• poor social skills

questions.title: applicants' inappropriate 【   】 and appearance
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 experience and skills、applicants；提示词 inappropriate，此处应填名词。
题目解析：在听到定位词 experience and skills 出现后，注意抓取符合题干的名词代入。提示词 inappropriate 以反义词形式 appropriate 出现，答案句为：“Wearing appropriate clothes and how they look during an interview are key to a successful interview.”其中 how they look 替换了题干中的 appearance。因此答案为 clothes。

transcript.en: Tutor: For today's session we're going to look at the job market and employers' hiring strategies.
transcript.cn: 今天这节课我们要来看看就业市场和雇主的招聘策略。

transcript.en: Tutor: It mainly takes great people to generate and execute efficient strategies and ideas.
transcript.cn: 要制定并执行高效的策略和创意，主要还得靠优秀的人才。

transcript.en: Tutor: In fact, a company's growth is strongly supported by its human resources.
transcript.cn: 事实上，一家公司的成长在很大程度上是靠它的人力资源支撑起来的。

transcript.en: Tutor: Therefore, we'll look at the determining factors for recruitment, the traits of a successful applicant and the possible reasons for rejection.
transcript.cn: 所以，我们会看看招聘的决定性因素、成功应聘者的特质，以及被拒绝的可能原因。

transcript.en: Tutor: First, let's take a look at the job market for students who have just graduated from secondary schools.
transcript.cn: 首先，我们来看看刚从中学毕业的学生所面对的就业市场。

transcript.en: Sansa: So, what should they focus on when applying for a job?
transcript.cn: 那么，他们在求职的时候应该主要突出什么呢？

transcript.en: Sansa: Is education the most important factor in recruitment?
transcript.cn: 学历是招聘中最重要的因素吗？

transcript.en: Tutor: Well, actually the degree is not that important.
transcript.cn: 嗯，其实学历没那么重要。

transcript.en: Tutor: It's not the only factor that personnel managers take into consideration.
transcript.cn: 它并不是人事经理唯一会考虑的因素。

transcript.en: Tutor: Some employees with years of work experience still have a competitive edge over fresh graduates with a higher educational degree.
transcript.cn: 有些拥有多年工作经验的员工，比起学历更高的应届毕业生，反而更有竞争优势。

transcript.en: Tutor: However, with neither an impressive educational background nor practical experience, secondary school graduates should stress that they have great enthusiasm for work.
transcript.cn: 不过，既没有亮眼的教育背景、又没有实践经验的中学毕业生，就应该强调自己对工作有极大的热情。

transcript.en: Tutor: Employees who are interested in their job often work hard and are willing to take more responsibilities.
transcript.cn: 对工作感兴趣的员工往往工作努力，也愿意承担更多责任。

transcript.en: Sansa: What else should they highlight during a job interview?
transcript.cn: 那他们在面试时还应该突出什么呢？

transcript.en: Tutor: Well, it is wise for them to demonstrate that they possess valuable characteristics such as curiosity, willingness to learn new stuff and good interpersonal skills.
transcript.cn: 嗯，明智的做法是展示出他们具备一些宝贵的特质，比如好奇心、愿意学习新东西以及良好的人际交往能力。

transcript.en: Tutor: Companies favour applicants who are easy going and fit in quickly.
transcript.cn: 公司偏爱那些随和、能很快融入的应聘者。

transcript.en: Sansa: Right.
transcript.cn: 对。

transcript.en: Sansa: But I guess things get harder for women to re-enter the workforce and reconnect after raising children at home.
transcript.cn: 不过我想，对于那些在家带孩子之后想重返职场、重新与工作接轨的女性来说，情况会更难一些吧。

transcript.en: Tutor: Absolutely.
transcript.cn: 确实如此。

transcript.en: Tutor: After taking an extended absence from work, women with young children have to strike a good balance between work and family.
transcript.cn: 在长时间离开工作之后，有年幼孩子的女性必须在工作和家庭之间找到一个好的平衡。

transcript.en: Tutor: Changes have to be made within the family to ensure they have sufficient working hours.
transcript.cn: 家庭内部得做出一些调整，才能保证她们有足够的工作时间。

transcript.en: Tutor: Apart from that, they are working with totally new colleagues in an unfamiliar workplace.
transcript.cn: 除此之外，她们要和全新的同事在一个陌生的工作环境里共事。

transcript.en: Tutor: After even two or three years of absence, they face challenges of rusty or obsolete skills.
transcript.cn: 哪怕只离开了两三年，她们也要面对技能生疏或过时的问题。

transcript.en: Tutor: Even though they're quite confident in themselves, they often find it hard to adapt to these changes.
transcript.cn: 尽管她们对自己相当有信心，但常常还是发现很难适应这些变化。

transcript.en: Tutor: But on the other hand, many employers find these mature women valuable assets in the workplace.
transcript.cn: 但另一方面，很多雇主觉得这些成熟的女性是职场里的宝贵财富。

transcript.en: Tutor: They are excellent role models for younger employees as being quite motivated and with a strong work ethic.
transcript.cn: 她们积极主动、有很强的职业道德，是年轻员工的绝佳榜样。

transcript.en: Sansa: Umm … Indeed, employers not only need qualified but also flexible and well-trained employees.
transcript.cn: 嗯……的确，雇主不仅需要有能力的员工，也需要灵活又训练有素的员工。

transcript.en: Sansa: Given the experience, perspectives and stability, these women are offered unique opportunities in today's workplace.
transcript.cn: 考虑到经验、视角和稳定性，这些女性在如今的职场中获得了独特的机会。

transcript.en: Tutor: Yes, exactly.
transcript.cn: 对，正是这样。

transcript.en: Sansa: So what kind of people do companies generally find unwelcoming?
transcript.cn: 那么公司一般会觉得哪类人不太受欢迎呢？

transcript.en: Sansa: Will they consider candidates from a rival company?
transcript.cn: 他们会考虑来自竞争对手公司的人选吗？

transcript.en: Tutor: Well, actually, hiring employees from a competing firm is not a taboo.
transcript.cn: 嗯，其实从竞争对手公司挖员工并不是什么禁忌。

transcript.en: Tutor: Highly skilled workers are always welcomed no matter where they're from.
transcript.cn: 高技能的员工无论来自哪里都是受欢迎的。

transcript.en: Tutor: What concerns employers the most is how they left the previous company that they had worked for years.
transcript.cn: 雇主最在意的是他们当初是怎么离开自己干了好几年的上一家公司的。

transcript.en: Tutor: If they have been fired, they must have done something wrong during the previous job.
transcript.cn: 如果他们是被开除的，那他们在上一份工作中肯定做错了什么。

transcript.en: Sansa: What about people who remain unemployed for a considerable period of time?
transcript.cn: 那么那些长时间处于失业状态的人呢？

transcript.en: Tutor: Well, this type of employee isn't at the bottom of the employment list.
transcript.cn: 嗯，这类求职者并不是排在录用名单最末尾的。

transcript.en: Tutor: The reason why they remain unemployed is the key.
transcript.cn: 关键在于他们为什么一直失业。

transcript.en: Tutor: Most companies cast doubts on applicants who left the job simply because they didn't like the working hours or the work assigned.
transcript.cn: 大多数公司会对那些仅仅因为不喜欢工作时间或分配的工作就辞职的应聘者心存疑虑。

transcript.en: Tutor: Have you read any articles about worker retrenchment?
transcript.cn: 你读过什么关于裁员的文章吗？

transcript.en: Sansa: Yes.
transcript.cn: 读过。

transcript.en: Sansa: Worker retrenchment on a massive scale is sometimes introduced as one of the short-term measures to resuscitate the industry through the reduction of overhead costs.
transcript.cn: 大规模裁员有时会作为一种短期措施推出，通过削减管理成本来重振某个行业。

transcript.en: Sansa: Workers are also laid-off as companies restructure and reform.
transcript.cn: 在公司重组和改革的时候也会裁掉一些员工。

transcript.en: Sansa: But what happens to these retrenched workers afterwards?
transcript.cn: 但这些被裁的员工之后会怎么样呢？

transcript.en: Sansa: Is it easy for them to find another job?
transcript.cn: 他们要再找一份工作容易吗？

transcript.en: Tutor: Well, generally companies are reluctant to hire them.
transcript.cn: 嗯，一般来说公司不太愿意雇他们。

transcript.en: Sansa: Why is that?
transcript.cn: 这是为什么呢？

transcript.en: Sansa: They must have plenty of work experience in the previous industry.
transcript.cn: 他们在之前的行业肯定有很丰富的工作经验。

transcript.en: Sansa: Isn't that a strength for them in the job market?
transcript.cn: 这在就业市场上难道不是一种优势吗？

transcript.en: Tutor: Actually, new industries are eager to hire experienced workers, but retrenched workers know nothing about other industries.
transcript.cn: 其实，新兴行业很想雇有经验的员工，但被裁的员工对其他行业一无所知。

transcript.en: Tutor: And it also costs employers more to retrain them.
transcript.cn: 而且重新培训他们也要花雇主更多的钱。

transcript.en: Tutor: They need training courses to acquire relevant skills and qualifications for a new career.
transcript.cn: 他们需要培训课程来获得新职业所需的相关技能和资质。

transcript.en: Sansa: Our government is providing public training programs to help them identify their current skills and acquire new skills to improve their career options.
transcript.cn: 我们政府正在提供公共培训项目，帮他们认清自己现有的技能、学习新技能，从而拓宽职业选择。

transcript.en: Sansa: Isn't that right?
transcript.cn: 不是这样吗？

transcript.en: Tutor: Yes.
transcript.cn: 是的。

transcript.en: Tutor: But retrenched workers are generally too old.
transcript.cn: 但被裁的员工普遍年纪偏大。

transcript.en: Tutor: They are less motivated and energized at work.
transcript.cn: 他们在工作中的积极性和精力都不如从前。

transcript.en: Tutor: Companies thus prefer younger employees to them.
transcript.cn: 所以公司会更倾向于雇用比他们年轻的员工。

transcript.en: Tutor: They are also reluctant to adjust and are unwilling to be retrained.
transcript.cn: 他们也不太愿意做出调整，不愿意接受再培训。

transcript.en: Sansa: Do retrenched workers from companies with big names have an advantage over other job applicants?
transcript.cn: 从大公司出来的被裁员工，相比其他求职者会有优势吗？

transcript.en: Tutor: No.
transcript.cn: 没有。

transcript.en: Tutor: Employers today value skills more than where they're previously from.
transcript.cn: 如今的雇主更看重技能，而不是你之前是从哪儿来的。

transcript.en: Sansa: Right.
transcript.cn: 对。

transcript.en: Sansa: So what are the most common factors of rejection during application?
transcript.cn: 那么在求职过程中最常见的被拒原因是什么呢？

transcript.en: Tutor: Applicants are rejected mainly for three reasons.
transcript.cn: 应聘者被拒主要有三个原因。

transcript.en: Tutor: First, they have experience and skills that are irrelevant to the industry.
transcript.cn: 第一，他们的经验和技能和这个行业不相关。

transcript.en: Tutor: Even experienced workers may receive rejection letters.
transcript.cn: 就算是有经验的员工也可能会收到拒信。

transcript.en: Tutor: Wearing appropriate clothes and how they look during an interview are key to a successful interview.
transcript.cn: 面试时穿得得体、以及看上去的形象，是面试成功的关键。

transcript.en: Tutor: No matter how good their presentation skills are, a shirt with stains or informal clothes like a pair of jeans only means the applicants aren't taking the interview seriously.
transcript.cn: 不管他们的表达能力有多好，一件有污渍的衬衫、或者牛仔裤这样的休闲装，都只会说明应聘者没把这场面试当回事。

transcript.en: Sansa: Yes, during an interview, formal dress is key to a good start and will inevitably affect how people evaluate you later.
transcript.cn: 是的，面试的时候，正装是良好开端的关键，而且必然会影响别人之后对你的评价。

transcript.en: Sansa: What's the third factor?
transcript.cn: 那第三个因素是什么？

transcript.en: Sansa: Let me guess, is it interpersonal skills?
transcript.cn: 让我猜猜，是人际交往能力吗？

transcript.en: Tutor: Yes.
transcript.cn: 对。

transcript.en: Tutor: Companies are eager to hire people who can work well with others and fit in quickly.
transcript.cn: 公司都很想雇用那些能和别人好好共事、又能很快融入的人。

transcript.en: Tutor: It's difficult to work in an environment full of conflicts.
transcript.cn: 在一个充满冲突的环境里工作是很难的。

transcript.en: Tutor: Therefore, people who don't get along well with others are often rejected.
transcript.cn: 所以，那些和别人处不好关系的人往往会被拒。

transcript.en: Sansa: I see …
transcript.cn: 我明白了……

groups.title: Organic Farming
groups.desc:
Questions 31-37
Complete the notes below.
Write ONE WORD AND/OR A NUMBER for each answer.
groups.collect: Advantages • Organic farming is $31$. • It can boost the $32$ of organic food products. • It can save necessary $33$ for producing pesticides and fertilisers. Comparison with traditional farming • $34$ which double in number may attack organic vegetables. • Production of organic vegetables such as tomatoes and $35$ is 40% lower. • Winter weed production is $36$ lower than it used to be. • Production of organic products for animals including organic $37$ keeps the same.

questions.title: Organic farming is 【   】.
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 Advantages；提示词 is，此处应填形容词。
题目解析：本题有一定难度，答案前置，于定位词 Advantages 之前出现。录音中先对有机农业做了简单介绍，接着通过 From this we can see 引出答案信息："… organic farming is a sustainable production system, which is one of its advantages."硬实力做题需要听懂内容，而技巧性做题可在听的过程中有意识地抓取符合预判的形容词，再通过随后出现的定位词进行确认。因此答案为 sustainable。

questions.title: It can boost the 【   】 of organic food products.
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 organic food products；提示词 the，此处应填名词。
题目解析：通过观察题干标题可知，第 31–33 题应该并列为有机农业的三个优势，听录音时，可利用语义的递进进行定位。本题录音中的并列信号词 Another benefit 提示我们答案即将出现："… the sales of organically produced food are promoted."其中 organically produced food 替换了定位词 organic food products，promoted 则替换了题干中的 boost。因此答案为 sales。

questions.title: It can save necessary 【   】 for producing pesticides and fertilisers.
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 pesticides、fertilisers；提示词 necessary，此处应填名词。
题目解析：本题同为有机农业的优势之一，注意听语义的递进。答案信息由并列信号词 What's more 引出："… organic farming can lower the demand for these fertilisers and pesticides, so it is conducive to the conservation of essential energy."其中定位词 pesticides 和 fertilisers 原词重现，conservation 替换了题干中的 save，essential 替换了 necessary。因此答案为 energy。

questions.title: 【   】 which double in number may attack organic vegetables.
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 double、organic vegetables；提示词 double，此处应填名词。
题目解析：本题较难，再次出现了答案前置，两个定位词都在答案之后才出现。通过观察标题和题干可知，第 34–37 题涉及有机农业和传统农业的比较，考查有机农业在这几方面发生了哪些变化，重点听有关变化的描述。小标题 Comparison 在录音中以 differs greatly from 出现，首先介绍了在 agricultural biodiversity（农业生物多样性）上的不同，先以 earthworms 为例进行了比较，出现了 three times as high as，不符合题干中的 double，可排除。接着以并列信号词 Moreover 引出另一个比较对象 insects，可先记下该词，随后听到："… which is twice as many as those in traditional farms."其中 twice as many as 替换了题干中的 double，而另一个定位词 organic vegetables 也紧跟着出现，录音用 be damaged 替换了题干中的 attack。因此答案为 insects。

questions.title: Production of organic vegetables such as tomatoes and 【   】 is 40% lower.
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 organic vegetables、tomatoes、40%；提示词 tomatoes、and，此处应填与 tomatoes 并列的名词。
题目解析：通过 The other main difference 是 crop yields（粮食产量）可知，接下来要谈论在这方面的不同了。本题不难，定位词 organic vegetables、tomatoes、40% 均原词重现，锁定答案句为："… organic vegetables like tomatoes and potatoes yielded are 40 percent less than conventional ones."其中 yielded 替换了题干中的 Production，less 替换了 lower，抓取与 tomatoes 并列的词汇即可。因此答案为 potatoes。

questions.title: Winter weed production is 【   】 lower than it used to be.
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 Winter、weed；提示词 is、lower，此处应填百分比。
题目解析：本题紧跟上一题，容易错过。在听到并列信号词 In addition 时就应集中注意力，接着定位词 weed 和 Winter 原词重现，但需要注意的是，录音中存在干扰信息 90 percent，这是目前的占比，而不是下降的百分比，录音中用 has decreased by … 替换了题干中的 is … lower，than before 替换了 than it used to be。因此答案为 10% 或 10 percent。

questions.title: Production of organic products for animals including organic 【   】 keeps the same.
questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 organic products、animals、same；提示词 organic，此处应填名词。
题目解析：本题难度不大，定位词 organic products 和 animals 原词重现，答案句为："… organic products are cultivated not only for humans but also for animals, e.g., organic grass."其中 e.g. 替换了题干中的 including，organic 所修饰的词汇即为答案。另一个定位词 same 的表述出现得较晚，在后文中被替换为 constant。因此答案为 grass。

groups.desc:
Questions 38-40
Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

questions.title: What happened to organic farming in India?

options.title: B
options.content: Farmers were reluctant to accept it.

options.title: C
options.content: Farmers could apply it successfully by following instructions.

questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 India。
题目解析：定位词 India 出现得较早，需耐心往下听。首先听到选项 C 中的 apply、successfully 和 instructions，但用的是否定表达 could not，可知有机农业并未成功应用，故排除。两三句后出现了答案句："… and the production of crops kept growing year on year."其中 production 替换了选项 A 中的 Yields（产量），kept growing 替换了 increased。而选项 B，录音中只在开始提到农民比较熟悉传统农业方式，并未提及是否愿意接受有机农业。因此答案为 A。

questions.title: Conclusion in an international organic farming review focuses on

options.title: A
options.content: whether people are interested in organic farming.

options.title: B
options.content: comparing different methods of organic farming in different countries.

options.title: C
options.content: whether organic farming is beneficial to wildlife.

questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 Conclusion、international；提示词 focuses on。
题目解析：注意题干问的是这篇 review 结论部分所关注的对象。定位词 international 原词重现，接着听到了选项 A 中的 interested in，但这属于 the first part（开头部分）的内容，故排除。随后出现了转折信号词 But，引出这篇 review 的重点或主干部分，即 the volume of the investigation the author did in different countries，注意与选项 B 区分，录音说的是在不同国家采取的调查数量，选项 B 说的是在不同国家有机农业的种植方式，故排除。答案句由表示结果的信号词 Therefore 引出："Therefore, the last part of the review is mainly talking about whether organic farming brings positive effects to animals."其中 the last part 即题干中的 Conclusion，is mainly talking about 替换了题干中的 focuses on，brings positive effects to 替换了选项 C 中的 is beneficial to，animals 替换了选项 C 中的 wildlife。因此答案为 C。

questions.title: According to the speaker, what is the main advantage of organic farming?

options.title: B
options.content: It can produce food of more nutritional value.

questions.analysis:
听前预测：定位词 main advantage；提示词 speaker。
题目解析：提示词暗示答案应为 speaker 自己的观点。定位词出现后，先后听到了选项 B 和 C，不过这两个都不是 speaker 的观点，可排除。speaker 自己的观点由转折信号词 However 引出，通过 as far as I'm concerned 可判断其后出现的观点即为答案信息："… it's you – the ones who buy organic products – that will decide what they can bring to you because market demand determines everything."其中 the ones who buy organic products 和 market demand 替换了选项 A 中的 consumers，decide 和 determines 替换了 depends on。因此答案为 A。

transcript.en: Hi everyone, what I'm going to talk about today is organic farming.
transcript.cn: 大家好，我今天要讲的是有机农业。

transcript.en: Let's first look at what organic farming is.
transcript.cn: 我们先来看看什么是有机农业。

transcript.en: It can be defined as one or more systems of production, which don't use synthetic fertilisers, man-made pesticides, herbicides, antibiotics or livestock feed additives to grow crops and raise animals.
transcript.cn: 它可以定义为一种或多种生产系统，在种植作物和饲养动物时不使用合成肥料、人造农药、除草剂、抗生素或牲畜饲料添加剂。

transcript.en: Virtually all organic farmers prefer to use renewable resources and recycling to give what conventional farmers might consider waste back to the soil of Mother Nature.
transcript.cn: 几乎所有有机农户都倾向于使用可再生资源和循环利用，把传统农户可能视为废料的东西还给大自然的土壤。

transcript.en: From this we can see organic farming is a sustainable production system, which is one of its advantages.
transcript.cn: 由此我们可以看出，有机农业是一种可持续的生产系统，这是它的优势之一。

transcript.en: Organic farmers use numerous techniques to promote the quality of life of plants and animals within existing ecosystems and minimise pollution that occurs with all conventional farming practices.
transcript.cn: 有机农户采用多种技术，在现有生态系统内提升动植物的生存质量，并把所有传统耕作方式都会带来的污染降到最低。

transcript.en: Another benefit is that, with the increasing consumer demand for a healthier, more nutritionally-balanced product, the sales of organically produced food are promoted.
transcript.cn: 另一个好处是，随着消费者对更健康、营养更均衡产品的需求不断增加，有机食品的销量也被带动起来了。

transcript.en: According to the research, it has become the fastest growing sector of agriculture and has shown an annual increase of at least 20 percent during the last 6 years.
transcript.cn: 据研究，它已经成为农业中增长最快的领域，在过去6年里每年至少增长20%。

transcript.en: What's more, both chemical fertilisers and synthetic pesticides consume non-renewable resources such as oil and coal, but the development of organic farming can lower the demand for these fertilisers and pesticides, so it is conducive to the conservation of essential energy.
transcript.cn: 更重要的是，化肥和合成农药都会消耗石油、煤炭这类不可再生资源，而有机农业的发展可以降低对这些化肥和农药的需求，因此有利于节约必需的能源。

transcript.en: At the same time, it also can reduce industrial pollution caused by the production of these chemical substances.
transcript.cn: 同时，它还能减少生产这些化学物质所造成的工业污染。

transcript.en: Organic farming differs greatly from the traditional one in a number of ways.
transcript.cn: 有机农业在很多方面都和传统农业有很大不同。

transcript.en: Let's discuss it from the perspective of agricultural biodiversity firstly.
transcript.cn: 我们先从农业生物多样性的角度来讨论一下。

transcript.en: For instance, earthworms are an important indicator of soil fertility.
transcript.cn: 比如说，蚯蚓是土壤肥力的一个重要指标。

transcript.en: Studies have shown that the density and species number of earthworms in organically managed soils are three times as high as those in conventional fields.
transcript.cn: 研究表明，有机方式管理的土壤中蚯蚓的密度和种类数量是传统农田的三倍。

transcript.en: Moreover, the biodiversity in organic farming can also be reflected in the number of insects, which is twice as many as those in traditional farms.
transcript.cn: 而且，有机农业的生物多样性还体现在昆虫的数量上，是传统农场的两倍。

transcript.en: This might be highly related to the fact that organic vegetables are grown without any human-made pesticides, making these vegetables more likely to be damaged.
transcript.cn: 这可能和有机蔬菜在种植过程中不使用任何人造农药密切相关，这使得这些蔬菜更容易受到虫害。

transcript.en: The other main difference between organic and traditional farming lies in crop yields, one of the biggest concerns of organic farmers.
transcript.cn: 有机农业和传统农业的另一个主要区别在于作物产量，这也是有机农户最关心的问题之一。

transcript.en: Organic farms, though possibly well adapted for certain local environments, produce less food per unit of land.
transcript.cn: 有机农场虽然可能很适应某些当地的环境，但单位面积的粮食产量更低。

transcript.en: For example, organic vegetables like tomatoes and potatoes yielded are 40 percent less than conventional ones.
transcript.cn: 比如说，番茄和土豆这类有机蔬菜的产量比传统蔬菜低40%。

transcript.en: In addition, research has also found the number of weeds produced in winter is about 90 percent at present, which has decreased by 10 percent than before.
transcript.cn: 此外，研究还发现冬季杂草的产量目前大约是90%，比以前下降了10%。

transcript.en: However, lower crop yields in organic farming are still inevitable at the moment.
transcript.cn: 不过，有机农业作物产量偏低目前仍然是不可避免的。

transcript.en: Anyway, organic farming is a new direction for agricultural development.
transcript.cn: 总之，有机农业是农业发展的一个新方向。

transcript.en: These days a variety of organic products are cultivated not only for humans but also for animals, e.g., organic grass.
transcript.cn: 如今，各种有机产品的种植不仅是为了人类，也是为了动物，比如有机牧草。

transcript.en: It is commonly used in organic farms to feed cows.
transcript.cn: 它常被用在有机农场里喂牛。

transcript.en: Because of the non-use of synthetic fertilisers or pesticides, it can protect cows from diseases brought by large-scale breeding, thus ensuring the quality of raw milk.
transcript.cn: 由于不使用合成肥料或农药，它能保护奶牛免受大规模养殖带来的疾病，从而确保生乳的质量。

transcript.en: But an interesting phenomenon is that its production has remained constant these years, which is quite unique compared with other types of crops.
transcript.cn: 但有意思的一个现象是，它的产量这些年一直保持稳定，这和其他类型的作物相比是相当独特的。

transcript.en: Research on organic farming has covered various aspects and involved many countries.
transcript.cn: 关于有机农业的研究涵盖了各个方面，也涉及许多国家。

transcript.en: According to one study, organic farming was proposed in India in the early 1960s, when it was faced with acute shortages of food grains due to the ever-increasing population and natural disasters.
transcript.cn: 据一项研究显示，有机农业早在20世纪60年代初就在印度被提出，当时印度由于人口不断增长和自然灾害，正面临着严重的粮食短缺。

transcript.en: Although it could not be applied successfully at first, as farmers had been familiar with conventional instructions, proponents of organic farming claimed that organic agriculture emphasised biodiversity, and effective soil management could enhance the capacity to mitigate and even reverse the effects of climate change.
transcript.cn: 虽然一开始它没能成功推行，因为农民早已熟悉传统的耕作方法，但有机农业的支持者声称，有机农业强调生物多样性，而有效的土壤管理能够增强缓解甚至逆转气候变化影响的能力。

transcript.en: So, India needed organic farming along with modern scientific agriculture.
transcript.cn: 所以，印度既需要有机农业，也需要现代科学农业。

transcript.en: The study shows that in 2016 India became the country with the largest number of organic products, and the production of crops kept growing year on year.
transcript.cn: 这项研究显示，2016年印度成为有机产品数量最多的国家，作物产量也逐年增长。

transcript.en: Another international review about organic farming presents in the first part that people today have become increasingly interested in organic food, and this opinion is quite the same as that of other articles.
transcript.cn: 另一篇关于有机农业的国际综述在第一部分指出，如今人们对有机食品越来越感兴趣，这个观点和其他文章基本一致。

transcript.en: But what impressed me most was the volume of the investigation the author did in different countries.
transcript.cn: 但最让我印象深刻的是作者在不同国家所做调查的规模。

transcript.en: He conducted a survey in 99 countries, of which 66 are convinced that organic farming is beneficial to animals, while 8 hold the opposite opinion, and the remaining 25 say it is inconclusive.
transcript.cn: 他在99个国家做了一项调查，其中66个国家确信有机农业对动物有益，8个国家持相反意见，剩下的25个国家则表示尚无定论。

transcript.en: Therefore, the last part of the review is mainly talking about whether organic farming brings positive effects to animals.
transcript.cn: 因此，这篇综述的最后一部分主要讨论的是有机农业是否会给动物带来积极的影响。

transcript.en: We can see there are different views about the advantages of organic farming.
transcript.cn: 我们可以看出，关于有机农业的优势有不同的看法。

transcript.en: Some may think it can provide more nutritious and healthier food; others may say it can protect the natural environment and benefit wildlife.
transcript.cn: 有人可能认为它能提供更有营养、更健康的食品；也有人可能会说它能保护自然环境、有益于野生动物。

transcript.en: However, as far as I'm concerned, it's you - the ones who buy organic products - that will decide what they can bring to you because market demand determines everything.
transcript.cn: 不过，在我看来，是你们——也就是购买有机产品的人——来决定它们能给你们带来什么，因为市场需求决定一切。

transcript.en: OK, that's all I want to say, now let's open it up for discussion.
transcript.cn: 好，我要说的就是这些，现在我们来展开讨论吧。
